data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-24T01:34:27.496Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically regarding synchronous LINQ operations.

This is a knowledge question about EF Core and the Cosmos DB provider. Let me recall what I know:

In EF Core, the Azure Cosmos DB provider has been moving away from supporting synchronous operations. Here's the timeline:

- In EF Core 9 (.NET 9), synchronous operations on the Cosmos DB provider started throwing exceptions by default, but you could opt back in with `EnableSyncOverAsync()` or similar configuration.


- In .NET 10 (EF Core 10), the sync-over-async opt-in was still available but deprecated, which is why the user had it configured.
- In .NET 11 (EF Core 11), that opt-in support would be completely removed, breaking any code that relied on synchronous operations against Cosmos DB.

So the breaking change is that any synchronous LINQ queries or operations against the Cosmos DB provider that worked in .NET 10 with the opt-in enabled will simply fail in .NET 11 because the feature no longer exists. All synchronous LINQ operations will throw `InvalidOperationException` since the Cosmos DB provider only supports async operations, so I need to convert them to their async equivalents like `ToListAsync()`, `FirstAsync()`, `CountAsync()`, and `SaveChangesAsync()`.
